---
title: "Solutions — Exercise Sheet 4: Angular Momentum and Electron Spin"
subtitle: "EP4: Experimental Physics 4 — Atomic and Molecular Physics"
---

## Solution 1: Angular Momentum Algebra

**(a)** $[\hat{L}_z, \hat{L}_+] = [\hat{L}_z, \hat{L}_x + i\hat{L}_y] = i\hbar\hat{L}_y + i(-i\hbar\hat{L}_x) = \hbar(\hat{L}_x + i\hat{L}_y) = +\hbar\hat{L}_+$

Similarly: $[\hat{L}_z, \hat{L}_-] = -\hbar\hat{L}_-$. ✓

**(b)** $\hat{L}_z(\hat{L}_{\pm}|\ell,m\rangle) = ([\hat{L}_z,\hat{L}_{\pm}] + \hat{L}_{\pm}\hat{L}_z)|\ell,m\rangle = (\pm\hbar\hat{L}_{\pm} + m\hbar\hat{L}_{\pm})|\ell,m\rangle = (m\pm 1)\hbar\,\hat{L}_{\pm}|\ell,m\rangle$

So $\hat{L}_{\pm}|\ell,m\rangle$ is an eigenstate of $\hat{L}_z$ with eigenvalue $(m\pm 1)\hbar$. ✓

**(c)** For $\ell = 2$: $m = -2, -1, 0, 1, 2$.

$\langle L_x \rangle = \frac{1}{2}\langle 2,1|(\hat{L}_+ + \hat{L}_-)|2,1\rangle = 0$ (since $\hat{L}_{\pm}$ changes $m$ by $\pm 1$).

$\langle L_x^2 \rangle = \frac{1}{2}(\langle L^2\rangle - \langle L_z^2\rangle) = \frac{1}{2}(6\hbar^2 - \hbar^2) = \frac{5}{2}\hbar^2$

(using $\langle L_x^2\rangle = \langle L_y^2\rangle$ by symmetry and $L^2 = L_x^2 + L_y^2 + L_z^2$).

## Solution 2: Spin-½ and Pauli Matrices

**(a)** $\sigma_x^2 = \begin{pmatrix}0&1\\1&0\end{pmatrix}\begin{pmatrix}0&1\\1&0\end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix}1&0\\0&1\end{pmatrix} = \mathbf{1}$ ✓

Similarly for $\sigma_y^2$ and $\sigma_z^2$.

**(b)** $\sigma_x\sigma_y = \begin{pmatrix}0&1\\1&0\end{pmatrix}\begin{pmatrix}0&-i\\i&0\end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix}i&0\\0&-i\end{pmatrix} = i\sigma_z$

$\sigma_y\sigma_x = \begin{pmatrix}0&-i\\i&0\end{pmatrix}\begin{pmatrix}0&1\\1&0\end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix}-i&0\\0&i\end{pmatrix} = -i\sigma_z$

$[\sigma_x,\sigma_y] = 2i\sigma_z$, so $[\hat{S}_x,\hat{S}_y] = \frac{\hbar^2}{4}\cdot 2i\sigma_z = i\hbar\cdot\frac{\hbar}{2}\sigma_z = i\hbar\hat{S}_z$ ✓

**(c)** $P(S_z = +\hbar/2) = |1/\sqrt{3}|^2 = 1/3$.

$\langle S_x\rangle = \frac{\hbar}{2}\langle\chi|\sigma_x|\chi\rangle = \frac{\hbar}{2}\cdot\frac{1}{3}\begin{pmatrix}1&\sqrt{2}\end{pmatrix}\begin{pmatrix}0&1\\1&0\end{pmatrix}\begin{pmatrix}1\\\sqrt{2}\end{pmatrix} = \frac{\hbar}{2}\cdot\frac{1}{3}(\sqrt{2}+\sqrt{2}) = \frac{\hbar\sqrt{2}}{3}$

## Solution 3: Addition of Angular Momenta

**(a)** For $\ell_1 = 1$, $\ell_2 = 1$: $L = |1-1|, \ldots, 1+1 = 0, 1, 2$.

**(b)** Total states from uncoupled basis: $(2\cdot1+1)(2\cdot1+1) = 9$.

Coupled basis: $(2\cdot0+1) + (2\cdot1+1) + (2\cdot2+1) = 1 + 3 + 5 = 9$ ✓

**(c)** Normalization check:

$$
\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{6}}\right)^2 + \left(\sqrt{\frac{2}{3}}\right)^2 + \left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{6}}\right)^2 = \frac{1}{6} + \frac{2}{3} + \frac{1}{6} = 1 \quad\checkmark
$$

## Solution 4: Magnetic Moment and Spin Precession

**(a)** $\mu_B = e\hbar/(2m_e) = 9.274 \times 10^{-24}\;\text{J/T} = 5.788 \times 10^{-5}\;\text{eV/T}$

**(b)** $\Delta E = g_s\mu_B B_0 = 2 \times 5.788 \times 10^{-5} \times 1 = 1.158 \times 10^{-4}\;\text{eV}$

**(c)** The Larmor precession frequency:

$$
\nu = \frac{g_s\mu_B B_0}{h} = \frac{\Delta E}{h} = \frac{1.158 \times 10^{-4}\;\text{eV}}{4.136 \times 10^{-15}\;\text{eV·s}} = 28.0\;\text{GHz}
$$

This is the ESR resonance frequency at 1 T.
